rationale.D1: Confirmed two thin service interfaces (LimelightService with HttpLimelightService impl, PoseProvider implemented by LimelightSubsystem), but DriveSubsystem directly instantiates TalonFX/AHRS hardware with no IO interface and no Sim impl — not a full IO layer across mechanisms.
rationale.D2: Confirmed standard command-based binding in RobotContainer (button-to-command, DriveCommand as default), no Superstructure coordinator, no FSM, no state enum — pure Level 1 command composition.
rationale.D3: No simulationPeriodic, no WPILib physics sim classes, no IOSim implementations found anywhere in src/; desktopSupport is set false in build.gradle.
rationale.D4: Confirmed 4 real test files (AutoAlignTest, LimelightTest, IsAlignedTest, ParseJsonTest) with mockito-kotlin mocking and coroutine test dispatchers; CI workflow runs ./gradlew test on every push — real tests gated by CI.
rationale.D5: Confirmed SmartDashboard.putNumber/putBoolean calls in Robot.kt for match time, CAN utilization, limelight state; no AdvantageKit, DogLog, or Epilogue present — scattered dashboard telemetry only.
rationale.D6: Confirmed AutoBuilder.configure with PPHolonomicDriveController in DriveSubsystem, PathPlannerAuto used in RobotContainer, NamedCommands registered, and 11+ .auto files plus 13 .path files on disk — PathPlanner fully adopted.
rationale.D7: Confirmed Limelight polled via HTTP for tag-relative Pose3d (targetPose_RobotSpace) used for alignment servoing; no addVisionMeasurement, no SwerveDrivePoseEstimator — targeting only, not global pose estimation, though LimelightService interface adds abstraction.
rationale.D8: Confirmed real README with IDE/build instructions, detekt+ktlint+spotless configured in build.gradle and enforced in CI workflow that also runs tests and build on every push; 5 seasons tracked; library repo is a Python Romi training bot, not a reusable Kotlin library.
